skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Guides AI assistants in using the inference.sh CLI to generate, animate, upscale, merge, and add audio to videos with supported AI models.
skill.summary: Generate AI video workflows through the inference.sh CLI.
skill.author: skillssh

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are true positives because the skill authorizes and documents repeated Bash calls to the infsh CLI. I found no evidence of malware or prompt injection, but the CLI sends prompts, image URLs, audio URLs, and video URLs to an external inference service, so publication should include a network data sharing warning.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Sends User Media Data
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash(infsh *) and provides many infsh app run examples. This is an intended capability, but prompts and media URLs are passed to an external CLI and remote inference service, creating data disclosure and billing risk if users provide private assets.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and command examples directly show execution of the infsh CLI with user-provided prompts and URLs. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but the external processing risk is clear.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Example URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to inference.sh documentation, a hosted preview image, and placeholder media examples. I did not find evidence that these URLs exfiltrate credentials or contact unrelated domains.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links or placeholder input URLs used in examples. They still indicate network use, but not malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static high findings at the description and table header lines do not show cryptographic operations, hashing, encryption, or credential handling. No evidence found for weak cryptographic algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is skill metadata text and line 63 is a markdown table header. Neither location contains executable cryptographic code or a security-sensitive algorithm.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Access Limited to infsh Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants Bash access for infsh commands only. This reduces general shell exposure, but still allows networked CLI jobs that may process private prompts and media.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools value and examples consistently reference infsh commands. The pattern is intentional and scoped, but it remains an external command pattern.

content.value_statement: Creating AI video often requires choosing the right model and CLI inputs. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear workflows for video generation, animation, upscaling, lipsync, and foley tasks.
actual_capabilities: Lists supported text-to-video, image-to-video, avatar, lipsync, upscaling, foley, and media merger apps.
Provides infsh CLI examples for Google Veo, Grok Imagine Video, Wan, Seedance, OmniHuman, Fabric, and PixVerse.
Shows how to pass prompts, image URLs, audio URLs, and video URLs into supported video models.
Guides users to browse all video apps with the inference.sh CLI.
Points users to related skills for image generation, text-to-speech, avatars, and social posting workflows.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated inference.sh account.
Outputs depend on remote model availability, pricing, rate limits, and provider policies.
Private prompts and media URLs may be sent to external services for processing.
The skill provides CLI guidance, not a local video model or offline renderer.

use_cases.title: Create Social Video Drafts
use_cases.description: Generate short video concepts from prompts for posts, ads, and campaign tests.

use_cases.title: Animate Product Images
use_cases.description: Turn product stills into motion clips for launch pages and demo reels.

use_cases.title: Build Avatar Narration Clips
use_cases.description: Combine portrait and audio inputs to create talking head or lipsync videos.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a short AI video from this idea: [describe scene]. Use a fast model and explain what input you need before running it.

prompt_templates.title: Animate an Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this image URL to create a moving video: [image URL]. Ask me for motion style, duration, and output quality before choosing a model.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Talking Avatar
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking head video from this portrait URL and audio URL. Compare the best avatar and lipsync options before running the command.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multi-Step Video Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a complete AI video pipeline for [goal]. Include image generation, video generation, foley, upscaling, merging, and final delivery steps.

output_examples.input: A marketer asks for a five second beach sunset video with waves and warm lighting.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a text-to-video model, confirms duration and audio needs, then prepares an inference.sh CLI run with the scene prompt.

output_examples.input: A designer provides a product image URL and asks for a smooth rotating product clip.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects an image-to-video model, asks for aspect ratio and motion speed, and prepares the required image URL input.

output_examples.input: A training creator provides a portrait and narration file for an instructor avatar.
output_examples.output: The assistant compares avatar and lipsync models, warns about external media processing, and prepares the best matching workflow.

best_practices: Confirm model choice, duration, aspect ratio, and audio needs before running a remote job.
Use public or approved media URLs only when sending assets to external inference services.
Start with fast models for drafts, then switch to higher quality models for final renders.
anti_patterns: Do not send confidential media, private voices, or unreleased product footage without approval.
Do not run costly generation jobs before confirming user intent and expected output settings.
Do not assume one model fits every task; match text, image, avatar, foley, or upscaling needs.

faq.question: Does this skill generate videos locally?
faq.answer: No. It guides an assistant to use the inference.sh CLI, which runs remote AI video apps.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What kinds of video tasks are covered?
faq.answer: It covers text-to-video, image-to-video, avatar animation, lipsync, foley audio, upscaling, and media merging.

faq.question: Do I need an account?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill expects the inference.sh CLI to be installed and authenticated before running jobs.

faq.question: Can I use private media URLs?
faq.answer: Use only media that you are allowed to send to external services. Private assets may be processed remotely.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The command use is intentional, but it sends prompts and media URLs to external services through a CLI.
